##NAMES=Hydropascoite
##RRUFFID=R250067
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_3_(V_10_O_28_)·24H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Packrat mine, near Gateway, Mesa County, Colorado, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Dark yellow green blades on asphaltum associated with montroseite- and corvusite-bearing sandstone. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2017)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250067
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_2.69_Na_0.30_)(H_0.31_V^5+^_10_O_28_)·24H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.087 b: 11.0708 c: 21.8112 alpha: 94.112 beta: 96.053 gamma: 116.398 volume: 2150.2 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
